C:\Users\Padmanabh\OneDrive\Documents\S10_Comp_Desktop\code\.venv\Lib\site-packages\fastapi\testclient.py:1: StarletteDeprecationWarning: Using `httpx` with `starlette.testclient` is deprecated; install `httpx2` instead.
    from starlette.testclient import TestClient as TestClient  # noqa

-- Docs: https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/how-to/capture-warnings.html
25 passed, 1 warning in 11.34s
Typed USER_QUERY already goes through the same POST /api/run path as presets. The failures in your screenshot were from routing and cascade fallthrough, not from the UI ignoring custom text.
What was going wrong
1. Planner metadata gaps — For some typed queries, the planner set a vague metadata.question but not a full goal / app / electron_debugging_port, so the computer skill didn’t know it was a Cursor task.
2. Wrong cascade path — When electron failed (Cursor debug port not attached), the skill fell through to vision and hit gateway 503 — misleading and wrong for Cursor/Calculator tasks.
3. OneDrive file locks — graph.json rename errors (fixed earlier with retry/fallback in _atomic_write).
What we changed
Query-aware computer routing1:
• enrich_computer_metadata() infers from USER_QUERY:
• Calculator → app: Calculator
• Cursor → app: Cursor, electron_debugging_port: 9222
• Canvas → app: browser
• Applied automatically in skills.py before every computer run.
Smarter cascade2:
• Cursor tasks try electron on port 9222 even if the planner omits the port.
• If electron fails for Cursor, you get a clear error (run launch_cursor_debug.ps1) instead of falling through to vision.
• Calculator tasks no longer escalate to vision after hotkey failure.
Sessions path — AGENT_SESSIONS_ROOT in .env is now honored (uncomment to move sessions off OneDrive).
How to run your Cursor query
1. Gateway on :8110: cd llm_gatewayV10; .\run.ps1
2. Cursor debug: cd code; .\computer\scripts\launch_cursor_debug.ps1
3. cua-driver running
4. Type your query (or use the Cursor preset) → Run
You should see path=electron in progress, not path=vision.
Optional: avoid OneDrive locks
In code/.env, uncomment:
AGENT_SESSIONS_ROOT=C:\temp\agent-sessions
Restart the UI after changes.
